wine_museums.name: World Heritage Wine Museum (Világörökségi Bormúzeum)
wine_museums.description: Interactive museum at the confluence of the Tisza and Bodrog rivers exploring Tokaj's UNESCO World Heritage wine culture. Exhibitions include a 5D experience, a hot-air balloon virtual flight over the region, a mini-car vineyard drive, the Gönci Barrel Story. Curriculum spans the Tokaji Table multimedia installation as well.
wine_museums.admission: Adults HUF 1000; students/seniors HUF 500; families HUF 2500
permanent_collections: European World Heritage Wine Regions exhibition
Scent House and Wine Pharmacy sensory gallery

wine_museums.name: Tokaji Múzeum
wine_museums.description: Regional history museum in an 18th-century mansion built by Greek wine traders, with a 16th-century cellar beneath containing an Aszú sorting table and winemaking tools. Collections cover Tokaj-Hegyalja viticulture, Christian liturgical art, Judaica from the former Great Synagogue. Curriculum spans local folklore as well.
wine_museums.opening_hours: Contact museum for current hours; seasonal

wine_museums.name: Bormúzeum és Borászati Kultúra Háza Tolcsva
wine_museums.description: Wine museum in a 2 km mould-covered cellar on three levels dug into rhyolite-tuff in Tolcsva, some galleries dating to the 13th century. Houses more than 100,000 bottles; the oldest is an Aszú made in 1680. Part of the UNESCO World Heritage Tokaj wine region since 2002. Guided tours in English and Hungarian.
wine_museums.admission: HUF 1500 per adult; guided tour approx. 45 minutes

wine_museums.name: Rákóczi Castle Underground Wine Exhibition
wine_museums.description: Underground exhibition at the Rákóczi Castle Museum (Hungarian National Museum Rákóczi Múzeuma) dedicated to wine and viticulture of the Hegyalja. Displays old Tokaji bottles, labels, and early viticultural tools in the castle's vaulted cellars. Main building also covers Rákóczi family history.
wine_museums.opening_hours: Check museum website for current hours; seasonal
wine_museums.admission: Ticket includes Red Tower, chapel, and underground wine exhibition
permanent_collections: Rákóczi Castle wine and viticulture heritage

wine_museums.name: Hercegkúti Pincék World Heritage Cellar Village
wine_museums.description: The cellar rows of Hercegkút, a Swabian village 3 km from Sárospatak, form a living wine museum within the UNESCO World Heritage Tokaj-Hegyalja landscape. The Gombos-hegy and Kőporosi cellar rows, UNESCO-listed since 2002, are carved into rhyolite-tufa with distinctive Swabian black mould. Guided visits through Götz Pincészet.
wine_museums.opening_hours: Seasonal; guided visits via Götz Pincészet by appointment
wine_museums.admission: Via guided cellar tour; contact gotzpinceszet.hu
